title: Formula Field Modal with OpenAI Explanation
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to click on formula fields in the data dictionary and see an AI-generated plain-English explanation of the formula logic, so that I can understand complex Salesforce formulas without reading the raw code.
description:
Add a formula field indicator and detail modal to the data dictionary table. Formula fields display a green icon that reveals an interactive two-panel modal on click.

### Formula Indicator
- Every column backed by a Salesforce `calculatedFormula` displays a small green wrench icon
- Hover tooltip: "This is a formula field. Click to view details"

### Formula Detail Modal
**Left panel:** Formatted code view showing the raw `calculatedFormula` value from the Salesforce REST API.

**Right panel:** Text area (with copy-to-clipboard) displaying AI-generated output from two OpenAI prompts:

1. **Explain Formula** — Prompt: "I want explain how this Salesforce.com formula field called {fieldName} works: {calculatedFormula}". Returns a plain-English explanation of what the formula does.

2. **Check Description** — Prompt: "The description for a Salesforce field called {fieldName} is \"{fieldDescription}\". Does the following formula and description match: {calculatedFormula}". Validates whether the field's description accurately represents the formula logic.

### Additional Features
- **Update Description** button allows editing the field description from within the modal
- **Check Description** button is disabled (`disabled="disabled"`) when the description is null
- OpenAI functions should be abstracted into reusable classes to support future AI features across the app

### Edge Cases
- Adding the formula icon initially broke inline editing for the column; this was resolved during implementation
acceptance_criteria: Formula fields display a green icon indicator in the data dictionary table
Hovering the icon shows a tooltip: 'This is a formula field. Click to view details'
Clicking the icon opens a two-panel modal with raw formula on the left and AI output on the right
Explain Formula button sends the formula to OpenAI and displays a plain-English explanation
Check Description button validates formula-to-description alignment via OpenAI
Check Description button is disabled when the field description is null
Update Description button allows editing the description inline
Inline editing on formula columns continues to work after adding the icon
Copy-to-clipboard functionality works on the AI output panel
ux_changes: Adds a green formula indicator icon to data dictionary table columns backed by Salesforce formulas. Click opens a two-panel modal with formatted formula code on the left and AI-generated explanations on the right. Includes Explain Formula, Check Description, and Update Description buttons.
middleware_changes: New OpenAI integration service with reusable class architecture for sending prompts and handling responses. Two prompt templates: formula explanation and description validation.
api_changes: Reads `calculatedFormula` field from Salesforce REST API metadata. Sends formatted prompts to OpenAI API for formula explanation and description validation.
implementation_notes: OpenAI functions were designed as reusable classes to support future AI features across the app. The formula icon initially caused inline editing to break on the column, which was fixed. The field name in the prompt was initially hardcoded to '2 Digit NAICS Code' and needed to be made dynamic from the actual field name.
comment_summary: Ryan outlined the complete requirements with HTML mockups and two OpenAI prompt patterns. Waqar implemented all features including the formula icon, modal, and AI buttons. An inline editing regression was discovered and fixed. Ryan tested and validated everything except Check Description, which Waqar subsequently completed.
